title: Polynomial, Rational, and Nonlinear Models · Mastery Check
summary: Compare alternative growth and geometry models for a production process. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a nonlinear model comparison report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate polynomial degree, roots, multiplicity, end behavior, and turning points.
Fit and critique polynomial models without extrapolating unsupported behavior.
Interpret zeros, holes, asymptotes, and domain restrictions in rational models.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: A model passes through (4, 14) and (8, 26). Calculate its average rate of change.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (26 − 14) / (8 − 4).

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (26 − 14) / (8 − 4).
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units per input or per input.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Model Selection applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare nonlinear candidates using residual patterns, simplicity, mechanism, and validation.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Polynomial Shape to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Polynomial Shape.

checks.description: The calculated quantity is accurate to the stated tolerance and carries its requested unit.
checks.failureMessage: Recalculate from the documented model, check the scale, and include the requested unit.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using compare and nonlinear plus specific case evidence.
